mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and drives S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of periods where RSAFS growth is positive, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate, signaling a weakening labor market, typically precedes a decline in consumer confidence and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances following the trigger, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is high and housing starts are declining, it indicates tight monetary conditions impacting interest-sensitive sectors, leading to a contraction in economic activity and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 5.0% AND HOUST declines by more than 10% month-over-month
regime_hint: monetary tightening with housing market stress
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 shows positive 20-day returns in more than 45% of triggered periods, or if the correlation between the combined signal and returns is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong industrial production growth, indicating robust manufacturing and supply-side expansion, equity markets tend to react positively to sustained economic momentum, driving short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of triggered instances during identified expansionary regimes, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating Consumer Price Index, indicating rising inflation, erodes purchasing power and corporate profit margins, prompting central bank tightening and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL annual growth rate accelerates by more than 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 40% of periods following the trigger, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in non-farm payrolls reflects a healthy and expanding labor market, which underpins consumer spending and corporate revenues, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
regime_hint: any regime with positive employment growth
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of periods where PAYEMS growth is positive, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When both GDP growth and retail sales are strong, it signals robust aggregate demand and economic expansion, which typically translates into immediate positive sentiment and short-term S&P 500 gains over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annual growth > 3.0% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5%
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 shows negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of triggered periods, or if the combined signal does not correlate positively with returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts during an economic downturn indicates severe weakness in a key interest-rate sensitive sector, exacerbating recessionary fears and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 15% year-over-year
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances during identified recessionary regimes, the hypothesis is rejected.
